title_working: The Internet's Own Boy: The Story of Aaron Swartz
warnings: Claude owns research+script+design (left process); Codex begins at scenes/visuals/narration/music/motion/edit/render (right process). AI image gen = Codex generation (NOT SDXL); edit = Remotion + FFmpeg, local CPU libx264, quality-first.

FEATURE ~30 min = mid profile (runtime band 27-33). Narration ~4,889 [VO:] words (~28-29 min at ElevenLabs pace ~173 wpm); runtime reached with hero shots, factory b-roll, designed transitions, held memorial beats, and 4-part bookends.
OUTPUT GATE = docs/PD_ONE_PASS_PRODUCTION_SPEC.v2.md. Codex must satisfy rows 1-16 from the first render; not done until check_final_acceptance.py 23 exits 0. Self-asserted QC forbidden.

R3 + SENSITIVE (death by suicide). DEDICATED legal/rights + safe-handling review REQUIRED before publish. SAFE-HANDLING LOCK: death stated ONCE ('died by suicide on January 11, 2013, at age 26'); NO method/location/note/scene; never 'committed suicide'. NO single-cause narrative (causation attributed to the family statement). 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline on-screen + in description; CTA quiet/respectful. No real-person likeness.

WORDING LOCKS: co-authored RSS 1.0 (not invented); key collaborator on Markdown (not co-created); helped build CC technical layer (not founded); Reddit co-owner via Infogami merger (co-founder disputed). Charged, never tried or convicted. JSTOR settled civilly + did not want charges; MIT neutral. THE 35 YEARS = theoretical stacked statutory maximum, always paired with the ~6-month plea signal (Peters) and the Kerr critique. Aaron's Law proposed and NEVER passed; CFAA not meaningfully reformed.
